frame_analyses.description: Title screen for FLS Task 5: Intracorporeal Suture tutorial
Trainee manipulating needle driver outside laparoscopic trainer, loading needle with suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: External preparation of needle and suture

frame_analyses.description: Continuing to load and orient the needle in the needle driver
Final needle loading preparation before entering trainer
Instruments now inside trainer, approaching Penrose drain with needle
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean entry into working space, needle properly positioned

frame_analyses.description: Placing first suture through the Penrose drain at marked location
Beginning first throw - surgeon's knot with double wrap
Continuing first throw formation with double wrap
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good instrument coordination during knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Completing first throw and beginning second throw with hand switch
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching between throws observed

frame_analyses.description: Performing third throw with appropriate hand switching
Cutting suture ends with scissors inside trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper cutting technique maintaining appropriate suture tail length

frame_analyses.description: Task completed, final view of secured knot on Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean final result with well-positioned knot

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double wrap executed correctly
Single throw with proper hand switching from first throw
Final single throw with appropriate hand switch, knot appears secure

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well closed with no visible gaps after knot tightening

technique_summary: Well-executed intracorporeal suture with proper surgeon's knot formation and appropriate hand switching between throws. The knot appears secure and the drain closure is complete.
improvement_suggestions: Slightly faster knot tying could improve efficiency
Consider more precise suture placement to minimize deviation penalties
strengths: Excellent hand switching technique between throws
Proper surgeon's knot formation with double wrap
